title: Sense and Sensibility
description: Sense and Sensibility — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "理智与情感" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Allenham"
memes.description: 'The country estate of Mrs. Smith, Willoughby''s aunt — a property of substantial wealth whose inheritance Willoughby imperils through his seduction of Eliza Williams and his romantic entanglement with Marianne'

memes.label: "Appearance"
memes.description: "The visible surface of character and conduct — a deceptive but inescapable dimension of social judgment"

memes.label: "Attachment"
memes.description: "The emotional bonds of love, friendship, and family loyalty that drive the novel's central relationships"

memes.label: "Barton Cottage"
memes.description: 'The modest Devonshire home the Dashwood women rent from Sir John Middleton — a symbol of their reduced circumstances and the domestic setting for the novel''s central emotional dramas'

memes.label: "Barton Park"
memes.description: 'The Devonshire estate of Sir John Middleton, whose constant hospitality provides the social setting for the Dashwood sisters'' introduction to county society, Willoughby, and Brandon'

memes.label: "Berkeley Street"
memes.description: 'Mrs. Jennings''s London residence — a comfortable establishment where the Dashwood sisters stay during the London season, and the setting for Elinor''s painful interview with Lucy Steele'

memes.label: "Cleveland"
memes.description: 'The country estate of Mr. and Mrs. Palmer — the setting for Marianne''s near-fatal illness, where physical crisis catalyzes emotional transformation and the novel''s romantic threads begin their resolution'

memes.label: "Colonel Brandon"
memes.description: 'A retired military officer of thirty-five, quiet and honorable, whose steadfast devotion to Marianne Dashwood and tragic romantic history reveal the depth of feeling beneath his reserved exterior'

memes.label: "Combe Magna"
memes.description: 'John Willoughby''s estate in Somersetshire — a property of inherited wealth that enables his dissipated lifestyle and the financial pressures that drive him to abandon Marianne for a wealthy heiress'

memes.label: "Delaford"
memes.description: 'Colonel Brandon''s modest estate in Dorsetshire, whose living provides Edward Ferrars with an income and whose quiet dignity reflects Brandon''s own character — the site of the novel''s happiest resolutions'

memes.label: "Delicacy"
memes.description: "Refined sensitivity in feeling and conduct — a virtue that borders on vulnerability in Austen's moral vocabulary"

memes.label: "Devonshire"
memes.description: 'The English county where the Dashwood sisters relocate to Barton Cottage — a rural region whose distance from London and Sussex represents both geographical isolation and, paradoxically, social opportunity'

memes.label: "Disappointment"
memes.description: "The gap between expectation and reality — a recurring emotional experience that tests every character's resilience"

memes.label: "Duty"
memes.description: "Moral obligation — the tension between what characters owe to themselves, their families, and society"

memes.label: "Edward and Elinor's Marriage"
memes.description: "After four years of secret engagement to Lucy Steele and subsequent release, Edward Ferrars proposes to Elinor Dashwood at Barton Cottage; their marriage at Barton church in early autumn concludes the novel's central love story."

memes.label: "Edward Ferrars"
memes.description: 'Elder son of Mrs. Ferrars, a quiet and principled gentleman whose secret engagement to Lucy Steele and growing love for Elinor Dashwood form the novel''s primary romantic conflict'

memes.label: "Edward's Secret Engagement Revealed"
memes.description: "The revelation that Edward Ferrars has been secretly engaged to Lucy Steele for four years — a pivotal plot event that tests Elinor's composure and transforms the romantic stakes of the novel"

memes.label: "Elinor Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Eldest daughter of Henry Dashwood, embodiment of "sense" — prudent, restrained, and deeply feeling beneath a composed exterior. Central protagonist whose emotional discipline enables the novel''s happiest resolution.'

memes.label: "Estate"
memes.description: "Landed property as the foundation of identity, status, and social order in Austen's world"

memes.label: "Exeter"
memes.description: 'The Devonshire city where Mrs. Dashwood briefly contemplates relocating and where Edward Ferrars eventually takes orders — a secondary location representing the practical accommodations of modest gentry life'

memes.label: "Expectation"
memes.description: "The hopes and anticipations that shape character decisions — and the disappointments that follow when reality diverges"

memes.label: "Fanny Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Wife of John Dashwood and sister of Edward and Robert Ferrars — her cold economic logic persuades her husband to reduce his promised support for his half-sisters to nothing.'

memes.label: "Fortune"
memes.description: "Wealth, inheritance, and financial security — the invisible hand shaping every character's choices in Sense and Sensibility, from the Dashwoods' disinheritance to the final marriage settlements"

memes.label: "Harley Street"
memes.description: 'The London address of John Dashwood and Fanny — a fashionable street representing the mercenary values and social ambition that the novel critiques'

memes.label: "Independence"
memes.description: "The capacity to act and think autonomously — a value constrained by economic dependence and social expectation"

memes.label: "John Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Half-brother of Elinor and Marianne, inheritor of Norland Park — a fundamentally decent man whose weakness of character and deference to his selfish wife Fanny lead him to neglect his obligations to his stepmother and half-sisters'

memes.label: "John Willoughby"
memes.description: 'A handsome and charming gentleman whose romantic pursuit of Marianne Dashwood ends in betrayal, revealing the dangerous gap between sensibility and moral character'

memes.label: "Lady Middleton"
memes.description: 'Daughter of Mrs. Jennings, wife of Sir John Middleton — a cold, elegant woman whose only interests are her children and social propriety, embodying the emptiness of refinement without warmth'

memes.label: "London"
memes.description: 'The social and commercial capital where romantic illusions dissolve — the setting for Marianne''s devastating encounters with Willoughby, Lucy Steele''s triumph of manipulation, and the testing of every attachment formed in the countryside'

memes.label: "London Season"
memes.description: "Elinor and Marianne Dashwood accompany Mrs. Jennings to London for the winter social season, where Marianne eagerly anticipates reuniting with Willoughby — only to face his public rejection and the revelation of his engagement to Miss Grey."

memes.label: "Lucy Steele"
memes.description: 'A cunning and ambitious young woman of inferior social standing who has secretly engaged Edward Ferrars for four years, serving as the primary obstacle to Elinor''s happiness and embodying the dangers of social climbing'

memes.label: "Lucy's Marriage to Robert Ferrars"
memes.description: "Lucy Steele unexpectedly marries Robert Ferrars instead of Edward, freeing Edward from his secret engagement and enabling his union with Elinor Dashwood."

memes.label: "Margaret Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Youngest daughter of Henry Dashwood — a spirited thirteen-year-old whose romantic sensibility mirrors Marianne''s and whose presence reminds the reader of the generational stakes of the novel''s arguments about love and marriage'

memes.label: "Marianne Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Second daughter of Henry Dashwood, embodiment of "sensibility" — passionate, romantic, and emotionally transparent, whose journey from sensation to sense forms the novel''s emotional arc'

memes.label: "Marianne's Illness at Cleveland"
memes.description: "Marianne Dashwood's near-fatal putrid fever at Cleveland — the novel's turning point, catalyzing her emotional transformation and revealing the depth of Elinor's love"

memes.label: "Marriage"
memes.description: "The central social institution of Sense and Sensibility — an economic and emotional contract that determines the fate of every character, examined through prudence, passion, and moral integrity"

memes.label: "Move to Barton Cottage"
memes.description: "The Dashwood family's removal from Norland Park to a cottage in Devonshire, following Mr. Henry Dashwood's death and their displacement by John Dashwood and his wife Fanny."

memes.label: "Mr. Palmer"
memes.description: 'Husband of Mrs. Palmer (née Charlotte Jennings), a gentleman of fortune whose studied rudeness to everyone — particularly his wife — provides the novel''s sharpest portrait of marital indifference masked as wit'

memes.label: "Mrs. Dashwood"
memes.description: 'Widow of Henry Dashwood, mother of Elinor, Marianne, and Margaret — warm-hearted, impulsive, and prone to the same romantic sensibility as her daughter Marianne'

memes.label: "Mrs. Jennings"
memes.description: 'A wealthy widow, mother of Lady Middleton and Mrs. Palmer — good-humoured, vulgar, and indefatigably interested in matchmaking, whose genuine kindness offsets her lack of refinement'

memes.label: "Mrs. Palmer"
memes.description: 'Younger daughter of Mrs. Jennings, wife of Mr. Palmer — cheerful, good-natured, and utterly undaunted by her husband''s rudeness, embodying the resilience of natural good humor'

memes.label: "Norland Park"
memes.description: 'The Dashwood family estate in Sussex, inherited by John Dashwood after Henry Dashwood''s death — the wealthy home the Dashwood sisters must leave, setting the novel''s plot in motion'

memes.label: "Plymouth"
memes.description: 'The port city where Eliza Williams was seduced and abandoned by Willoughby — a distant location whose events cast a shadow over the novel''s romantic present'

memes.label: "Propriety"
memes.description: "The code of socially acceptable conduct — a standard that both protects and constrains Austen's characters"

memes.label: "Prudence"
memes.description: "Practical wisdom and cautious judgment in financial and social matters — a virtue tested by every character's choices"

memes.label: "Rank"
memes.description: "Social position and status — the invisible hierarchy that governs every interaction in Austen's world"

memes.label: "Robert Ferrars"
memes.description: 'Younger brother of Edward Ferrars, a vain and foolish coxcomb whose inherited fortune and eventual marriage to Lucy Steele illustrate the novel''s satiric treatment of undeserved privilege'

memes.label: "Sense"
memes.description: "Sound judgment, prudence, and rational self-command — the counterpoint to sensibility in Austen's moral scheme, embodied in Elinor Dashwood"

memes.label: "Sensibility"
memes.description: "The capacity for refined emotional response and acute feeling — a central theme in Sense and Sensibility, embodied most fully in Marianne Dashwood"

memes.label: "Sir John Middleton"
memes.description: 'A hearty, hospitable country gentleman and cousin to Mrs. Dashwood, whose love of company and sport provides the Dashwood sisters with Barton Cottage and a constant stream of social events'

memes.label: "Stanhill"
memes.description: 'The former home of Mrs. Dashwood and her daughters before their removal to Norland — a modest property whose sale provides part of the Dashwood women''s limited fortune'

memes.label: "Sussex"
memes.description: 'The home county of the Dashwood family, location of Norland Park — the place of origin and loss, from which the novel''s plot departs and to which it never returns'

memes.label: "Vanity"
memes.description: "Excessive pride in appearance, accomplishments, or status — a minor vice with major consequences in the novel's moral economy"

memes.label: "Willoughby's Betrayal of Marianne"
memes.description: "John Willoughby's public rejection of Marianne Dashwood in London — the emotional catastrophe that shatters her romantic ideals and forces her to reconsider her philosophy of sensibility"

memes.label: "Willoughby's Rescue of Marianne"
memes.description: "Marianne Dashwood falls and twists her ankle while walking near Barton Cottage; a handsome stranger, John Willoughby, carries her home — the fateful first encounter that ignites their passionate attachment."
